summary: Moved most of the common FallSpecial action out of asm into `src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_FallSpecial.c`, achieving a reported 92.68% file score. Fully matched functions included `ft_800968C8`, `ftCo_FallSpecial_Anim`, `ftCo_FallSpecial_IASA`, and `ftCo_FallSpecial_Coll`; `ft_80096900` and `ft_800969D8` were near-matches with inline asm guarded for `MUST_MATCH && !WIP`; `ftCo_FallSpecial_Phys` remained the weakest partial match at 79.88%. The PR also renamed the old address-based header `ftCo_0961.h` to action-based `ftCo_FallSpecial.h`, added motion-var layout for `fallspecial`, typed several helper prototypes, and added tooling support to compare against the WIP build path.

key_files.role: new C implementation for common FallSpecial functions, with matched C, partial C, and MUST_MATCH asm fallbacks
removed the decompiled functions from asm while keeping later remaining asm; exported `.L_80096D28` as `ftCo_80096D28`
tightened prototypes for helper functions used by FallSpecial
typed `ft_80083090` as a collision helper taking `ftCo_GObj*`, predicate, and event callbacks
added prototype for `un_80322598(int, float)` used during FallSpecial transition setup
added `ftCo_FallSpecial.c.o` to the text object list
changed score reporting to diff against `./build/wip/ssbm.us.1.2/` while retaining expected-root naming

what_changed: Added a 397-line `ftCo_FallSpecial.c` implementing the FallSpecial transition/setup, animation, IASA, physics, and collision functions.

Removed the corresponding asm bodies for `ft_800968C8`, `ft_80096900`, `ft_800969D8`, `ftCo_FallSpecial_Anim`, `ftCo_FallSpecial_IASA`, `ftCo_FallSpecial_Phys`, and `ftCo_FallSpecial_Coll` from `asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s`.

Kept difficult functions match-safe by wrapping inline asm versions of `ft_80096900`, `ft_800969D8`, and `ftCo_FallSpecial_Phys` under `#if defined(MUST_MATCH) && !defined(WIP)`, while providing readable C alternatives for WIP/non-must-match builds.
Replaced widespread includes of `ftCommon/ftCo_0961.h` with `ftCommon/ftCo_FallSpecial.h` across many fighter special-move files and ftCommon files.
Added `ftCo_FallSpecialVars` to `ftCommon_MotionVars`, mapping fields at fp+2340 through fp+2358 as `x0`, `x4`, `x8`, `xC`, `x10`, `x14`, and `allow_interrupt`.
Typed helper prototypes including `ftCo_800CC988`, `ftCo_800CCBE0`, `ftCo_800CEE70`, `ft_80083090`, and `un_80322598` to support the C translation.
Promoted the collision callback label `.L_80096D28` to the global symbol `ftCo_80096D28` so it could be referenced from C.
Added `ftCo_FallSpecial.c.o` to `obj_files.mk` before the remaining `ft_08A4.s.o` object.
Updated `tools/report_score.py` so asm-differ compares the WIP build object path instead of the expected object path.
smart_moves: Renaming the header from `ftCo_0961.h` to `ftCo_FallSpecial.h` made dependencies reflect the action domain rather than an address bucket, improving searchability and future ownership.
The PR isolated exact-match risk: straightforward functions were converted to C, while known-problem functions retained inline asm under must-match guards and readable C under WIP/non-MUST_MATCH.
Adding the motion-var union member documented the previously raw fp+2340..fp+2358 state used by FallSpecial and let the new C use `fp->mv.co.fallspecial` rather than raw offsets.

The C for `ft_80096900` and `ft_800969D8` reuses the same transition pattern: check `fp->x2224_flag.bits.b2`, call `Fighter_ChangeMotionState` with `ftCo_MS_FallSpecial`, initialize motion vars, ground/air transition helpers, then call `un_80322598` with spawn number and Y position.
The physics C splits behavior on `fp->mv.co.fallspecial.xC`, preserving the two drift/terminal-velocity paths visible in asm and using `ABS` to clamp drift magnitude in the second path.
The score report in the PR body makes partial-match status explicit, avoiding overclaiming: file 92.68%, but `ftCo_FallSpecial_Phys` only 79.88%.
decomp_lessons: When a file is only partially matched, move the tractable functions to C and retain guarded inline asm for hard cases rather than blocking all progress.
Action-specific headers are preferable to address-range headers once the function group is understood; update all consumers in the same PR to avoid duplicate declarations.
Before translating action code that uses fp+2340 motion variables, add a named `ftCommon_MotionVars` overlay so the implementation can express intent and preserve offsets.
For callbacks still living in asm, promote local labels to global named symbols before passing them from C.
Typed prototypes for helper functions can be a necessary matching step because call signatures affect generated code and make decompiled callers cleaner.
Score tables are useful for documenting what is actually matched: a high file score can still hide a weak function that needs later work.
A WIP C implementation can be kept alongside must-match asm to serve as documentation and a future matching target.

naming_conventions.lesson: Prefer action/module names over address-derived names like `ftCo_0961.h` when the function group has been identified.
Common action callbacks follow the established action-suffix convention.
When an asm-local callback label must be referenced from C, give it an `ftCo_` global symbol using its address.
The new header and dox changed the parameter name from `is_interruptable` to `allow_interrupt`; evidence only shows this spelling choice, not reviewer rationale.
Motion-vars structs can initially use offset-preserving placeholder member names (`x0`, `x4`, `x8`, etc.) when semantics are not fully known.

assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used `#if defined(MUST_MATCH) && !defined(WIP)` to keep inline asm bodies for functions that did not yet match in C.
Used `#pragma push` / `#pragma pop` and `asm void ... { nofralloc ... }` around inline asm blocks.
Kept readable C fallbacks for `ft_80096900`, `ft_800969D8`, and `ftCo_FallSpecial_Phys` outside the must-match asm path.
Inserted an `#ifdef MUST_MATCH` unused stack buffer in the C versions of `ft_80096900` and `ft_800969D8` with a `@todo Unused stack` note.
Documented an inline-related mismatch with `/// @todo Real solution is @c 0. Inline needs to be fixed.` and a temporary `#define SOLUTION 1` around `ft_80096900`.
Converted float constants from sdata2 labels in asm to literal `0.0f` and `1.0f` in inline asm/C context, removing `.L_804D85D0` and `.L_804D85D4` from the asm file.
Used `RETURN_IF(...)` chaining in `ftCo_FallSpecial_IASA` to express sequential interrupt checks while matching that function at 100%.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
The only human text body was the score report for `ftCo_FallSpecial.c`; there is no direct reviewer rationale to extract.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that fully match `ftCo_FallSpecial_Phys` or remove its inline asm fallback.
Find later PRs that replace placeholder fields in `ftCo_FallSpecialVars` with semantic names.
Find PRs that modify or explain the inline issue around `ft_80096900` and `SOLUTION`.
Find later users of `ftCo_80096D28` and whether it was eventually decompiled from `ft_08A4.s`.
Search for additional replacements of address-based headers like `ftCo_0961.h` with action-specific headers.
